summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #426 where Moltis behind a reverse proxy leaks the internal Docker port (13131) into the join URL displayed on the Nodes settings page.
summary.outcome: Applied a one-line frontend fix replacing `gon.get('port') || location.port` with just `location.port` in gatewayWsUrl(), added an e2e test that spoofs gon.port to verify the browser location port is always used, committed, pushed, and opened a PR.

repo: The project uses a `gon` pattern (server-injected JS data) to pass server-side config to the frontend; gon data is accessible via gon.get() and gon.set().
The web crate lives at crates/web/ with JS assets under src/assets/js/ and e2e tests under ui/e2e/specs/.
Biome is the formatter/linter for JS files; it must pass before committing.
The nodes settings page is served at /settings/nodes.

code.finding: gatewayWsUrl() previously used `gon.get('port') || location.port`, causing the internal bind port to leak into the join URL when behind a reverse proxy. Fixed to use `location.port` exclusively.
build_gon_data() always sets port to gw.port (internal bind port) regardless of MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY. This is a latent server-side issue but wasn't changed since the frontend fix was sufficient.

New test spoofs gon.port to 99999 via page.evaluate + dynamic import of gon.js, then re-navigates and asserts the displayed wss:// URL does not contain :99999 but does contain the browser's actual port.

workflow: The gon.js module can be dynamically imported inside page.evaluate() to call gon.set() for test spoofing — useful pattern for testing frontend/server data divergence.
Option B (simplest correct fix) was chosen over Option A (conditional logic) — browser location is always authoritative for the externally-visible URL.
Re-navigating after spoofing gon data is needed because the ConnectNodeForm renders on page load.

friction: The e2e test required a non-obvious approach: dynamically importing gon.js inside page.evaluate() to spoof the port, since gon is a module and not directly accessible as a global.
In the e2e environment gon.port and location.port are the same value, so a direct assertion without spoofing would not have caught the regression.
open_items: crates/web/src/templates.rs build_gon_data() still sets port to the internal bind port regardless of MOLTIS_BEHIND_PROXY — the server-side gon data is technically wrong behind a proxy, even though the frontend no longer uses it for the join URL.
The re-navigate approach in the test relies on gon persisting the spoofed value across navigation, which may be fragile depending on how gon state is initialized per page load.
